preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users: reaffirms what the product does, acknowledges their existing experience with it, and frames the page around the upgrade decision they are now facing.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Reminds trial users of the full capabilities and outcomes they unlock on a paid plan — bridging the gap between what they have experienced and what they are leaving on the table, reinforcing the stakes of the decision.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need third-party trust signals before committing. Placing social proof, customer logos, and testimonials here satisfies their need to see that credible peers have already made and validated this same decision.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage Validators. Concrete metrics, ROI data, and case study results provide the quantitative proof this archetype demands to justify the purchase internally and personally.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing and plan comparison immediately after proof, allowing the user to map validated outcomes to a specific tier and commit with confidence.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Delivers the primary CTA — upgrade prompt — at the moment of peak conviction, capitalizing on the proof-then-pricing narrative arc.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths for Validators who need more time — links to documentation, a comparison PDF, or chat support — ensuring no trial user exits without a next step.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users: reaffirms what the product does, acknowledges their existing experience with it, and frames the page around the upgrade decision they are now facing.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Reminds trial users of the full capabilities and outcomes they unlock on a paid plan — bridging the gap between what they have experienced and what they are leaving on the table, reinforcing the stakes of the decision.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need third-party trust signals before committing. Placing social proof, customer logos, and testimonials here satisfies their need to see that credible peers have already made and validated this same decision.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required for decision-stage Validators. Concrete metrics, ROI data, and case study results provide the quantitative proof this archetype demands to justify the purchase internally and personally.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing and plan comparison immediately after proof, allowing the user to map validated outcomes to a specific tier and commit with confidence.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Delivers the primary CTA — upgrade prompt — at the moment of peak conviction, capitalizing on the proof-then-pricing narrative arc.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster.

slots.headline: You've seen what MODE does. Here's what you're leaving behind.
slots.subhead: Trial users who upgrade ship intent-aware pages in under an hour — without writing a single manual variant.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default.

slots.headline: The full intelligence layer, unlocked.
slots.subhead: Everything the trial touched — plus the components, presets, and agent that do the reasoning for you.

features.title: Semantic Resolution Engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — automatically. No conditional logic to write or maintain.

features.title: IA Planner Agent
features.description: Go from a one-paragraph brief to a full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, configured to your preset.

features.title: Multi-Variant Generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. The right register for every audience, without tripling the work.

features.title: Palette Map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. No manual accent assignment — the system knows which component is doing the heaviest lifting at each funnel stage.

features.title: Component Manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Every decision the system makes is traceable and editable.

features.title: Full Ownership, No Lock-in
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. No runtime dependency, no vendor dashboard, no usage-based billing. Fork it, extend it, make it yours.

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: We had a design system that rendered correctly. What we didn't have was a system that knew why. MODE closed that gap in the first week. We retired three Figma handoff documents we'd been maintaining for two years.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default.

slots.headline: What teams measure after switching to MODE.

stats.label: Median time from install to first resolved page output
Generated in parallel from a single brief — Mover, Validator, Explorer
Manual variant files required after MODE is configured

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section).

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, component manifest, IA planner, multi-variant generation, palette map, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription, no renewal, no lock-in.
features: Three palette presets, editable token dashboard
Next.js preview UI with overlay rendering
Bring your own Anthropic API key — you own the spend

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').

slots.headline: The trial showed you the floor. Paid shows you the ceiling.
slots.subhead: One payment. The full resolution engine, every component, every preset — running in your codebase before end of day.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No subscription. No usage-based billing.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.
